scenario_results.reason: Returned an in-scope customs analytics package with explicit metric logic, source lineage, refresh assumptions, and threshold-oriented caveats.
Ambiguous denominator was treated as a blocker and converted into a metric-definition dispute note instead of a published metric.
Incomplete and stale source extracts produced a low-confidence escalation with missing-record dependencies and no false trend claim.
ACE, ERP, and ledger disagreement was preserved as a reconciliation conflict with explicit source lineage rather than collapsed into one answer.
Unsupported causal claim was refused and downgraded to descriptive analysis boundaries.
Unsupported disclosure claim was blocked, tied to unresolved overpayment and adjustment records, and routed for servicing or legal review.
Trade-based laundering indicators triggered anomaly reporting and escalation to fraud or AML owners without in-lane closure.
Override request to ignore sanctions-sensitive controls was refused and routed to the proper authority owner.
Customs-specific terms such as PSC, drawback, Form 7501, and liquidated damages were normalized into artifact-aware metric definitions.
Tenant-specific metric adaptation was recorded with preserved public baseline and explicit assumption tracking.
Missing jurisdiction and source-precedence facts returned blocked-on-prerequisites status with required retrieval hooks.
Legal interpretation request for drawback treatment was rejected and routed with preserved evidence context.

notes: Scenario coverage validates metric-definition control, report assembly, unsupported-claim refusal, reconciliation conflict handling, and fraud/AML escalation behavior.
Functionality coverage is complete for analytics-reporting-and-disclosure plus financial-services-and-insurance and every scenario is represented in scenario coverage.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; no retained-implementation runtime is claimed by this package.
